code: @Manual{,   title = {multibayes: Bayesian Multiplicity Adjustments},   author = {Margherita Calderan},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/mar-cald/multibayes}, }

title: Bayesian Multiplicity Adjustments
text: multibayes provides tools Bayesian inference decision-making across multiple parameters simultaneously. package offers two functions rooted different related principles: pd.adjust() addresses decision-level multiplicity problem arises Probability Direction (pd) used repeated decision rule, joint() provides simultaneous credible intervals faithful joint posterior distribution, enabling inference several parameters .

title: Bayesian Multiplicity Adjustments
text: functions operate directly posterior draws different modeling framework (e.g., brms, rstan, rstanarm).

title: Bayesian Multiplicity Adjustments
text: Probability Direction (pd) often used decision rule: hypothesis accepted pd exceeds threshold. decision made repeatedly across several hypotheses, family-wise error probability can much higher intended. pd.adjust() addresses decision-level problem making implicit prior probability global null explicit, following prior-odds framework Jeffreys (1938) Westfall et al. (1997). global prior probability hypotheses null, q, decomposed per-hypothesis prior: P(H0)=q1/mP(H_0) = q^{1/m} pd reweighted Bayes’ theorem: pdadj=pd⋅P(H1)pd⋅P(H1)+(1−pd)⋅P(H0)pd_{\text{adj}} = \frac{pd \cdot P(H_1)}{pd \cdot P(H_1) + (1 - pd) \cdot P(H_0)} parameters correlated, effective number tests meffm_\text{eff} (Cheverud, 2001) used place m, producing less conservative adjustment.

title: Bayesian Multiplicity Adjustments
code: library(multibayes)  # From a vector of pd values (independence assumed) pd_values <- c(H1 = 0.999, H2 = 0.946, H3 = 0.813, H4 = 0.763, H5 = 0.891, H6 = 0.987) pd.adjust(pd = pd_values, q = 0.4)  # Simulate correlated posterior draws  Sigma <- matrix(0.4, nrow = 6, ncol = 6); diag(Sigma) <- 1 mu    <- c(1, -0.1, 0.8, 0, 2, 3) draws <- MASS::mvrnorm(n = 4000, mu = mu, Sigma = Sigma) colnames(draws) <- c("H1", "H2", "H3", "H4", "H5", "H6")  # From posterior draws: pd and correlation estimated automatically pd.adjust(draws = draws, q = 0.4, R = TRUE)  # When draws are unavailable, supply an assumed mean correlation pd.adjust(pd = pd_values, q = 0.4, R = 0.4)

title: Bayesian Multiplicity Adjustments
text: data.frame one row per hypothesis:

title: Bayesian Multiplicity Adjustments
text: q encodes prior belief tested hypotheses simultaneously null. value 0.4 moderately skeptical default. Lower values lenient; higher values conservative. m reflect hypotheses directional claims made. Nuisance parameters (random effects, control covariates) counted.

title: Bayesian Multiplicity Adjustments
text: joint() computes equitailed credible intervals hold jointly across parameters: posterior probability every parameter lies within interval simultaneously equals requested level. stricter standard marginal credible interval, guarantees coverage parameter--parameter. Simultaneous coverage calibrated finding critical quantile cq fraction α\alpha posterior draws extreme cq least one parameter simultaneously.

title: Bayesian Multiplicity Adjustments
code: library(MASS)  mu    <- c(4, 0, -2) Sigma <- matrix(c(1, 0.8, 0.5,                   0.8, 1, 0.3,                   0.5, 0.3, 1), 3, 3) draws <- mvrnorm(2000, mu, Sigma) colnames(draws) <- c("theta1", "theta2", "theta3")  joint(draws, prob = 0.95)

what: Simultaneous credible intervals from joint posterior draws — joint
title: Simultaneous credible intervals from joint posterior draws — joint
text: Computes simultaneous equitailed credible intervals set parameters, guaranteeing parameters stay within bounds simultaneously user specified probability.

title: Simultaneous credible intervals from joint posterior draws — joint
Simultaneous credible intervals from joint posterior draws — joint
text: draws Numeric matrix data frame posterior draws (rows = draws, columns = parameters). prob Numeric scalar \((0, 1)\). Joint coverage probability (default 0.95). est.FUN Function point estimates (default median).

title: Simultaneous credible intervals from joint posterior draws — joint
text: dataframe : lower Lower bounds, one per parameter. est Point estimates via est.FUN. upper Upper bounds, one per parameter. prob Requested joint coverage probability. cq Critical value.

title: Simultaneous credible intervals from joint posterior draws — joint
text: Simultaneous coverage calibrated examining extreme draw across parameters jointly. draw, minimum tail probability across parameters computed. simultaneous threshold \(\alpha\)-quantile minima: \(\alpha\) draws extreme value least one parameter simultaneously. See Goeman et al. (2026) details. closely related implementation sim.cred.band credsubs package (Schnell et al., 2020).

what: Prior-odds adjustment for Probability of Direction (pd) — pd.adjust
title: Prior-odds adjustment for Probability of Direction (pd) — pd.adjust
text: function accepts either vector pre-computed pd values matrix posterior draws, pd values computed internally. global prior probability tested hypotheses null, \(q\), decomposed per-hypothesis prior \(P(H_0) = q^{1/m}\), \(m\) number hypotheses , correlation structure among parameters taken account, effective number tests \(m_{\text{eff}}\).

title: Prior-odds adjustment for Probability of Direction (pd) — pd.adjust
code: pd.adjust(pd = NULL, draws = NULL, q = 0.4, m = NULL, R = NULL)

title: Prior-odds adjustment for Probability of Direction (pd) — pd.adjust
text: pd Numeric vector pd values \([0.5, 1]\). draws Optional matrix data frame posterior draws (columns = parameters). provided, pd calculated automatically. q Numeric scalar \((0, 1)\): prior probability hypotheses null. Defaults 0.4. m Positive integer. number tested hypotheses. Defaults length(pd). Overridden R provided. R Optional correlation matrix posterior draws. Can provided directly matrix single scalar, computed automatically function posterior draws supplied (set R = TRUE). provided, \(m_{\text{eff}}\) calculated correlation structure used place m.

title: Prior-odds adjustment for Probability of Direction (pd) — pd.adjust
text: data.frame containing original pd, pd_adj, parameters q m used correction.

title: Prior-odds adjustment for Probability of Direction (pd) — pd.adjust
text: adjustment follows Bayes' theorem. Given per-hypothesis prior \(P(H_0) = q^{1/m}\) complement \(P(H_1) = 1 - P(H_0)\), adjusted pd : $$    pd_{adj} = \frac{pd \cdot P(H_1)}{pd \cdot P(H_1) + (1 - pd) \cdot P(H_0)} $$ R supplied, effective number tests \(m_{eff}\) estimated eigenvalues \(\lambda\) correlation matrix (Cheverud, 2001): $$    m_{eff} = K \left( 1 - \frac{(K-1) \text{Var}(\lambda)}{K^2} \right) $$ \(K\) number hypotheses.

title: Prior-odds adjustment for Probability of Direction (pd) — pd.adjust
